doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_get_version (mpfr/src/version.c L24-L28). Returns the upstream MPFR version string as a const char *. The TS port returns the same string verbatim.

This is a build-info accessor; no algorithm, no rounding, no precision. Tag minimums per Rule 7 inapplicable; single happy case (no-arg accessor pattern, same as mpfr_buildopt_*_p / mpfr_get_cputime).

The version string mirrors the upstream value at the cloned MPFR_VERSION (see mpfr/VERSION). Worklog 019 landed the string-output codec path that this function uses on the wire.
divergence_from_c:
Two divergences:

  1. Return type: C `const char *` -> TS `string`. The TS surface uses native JS strings for static text returns (no need to expose a c-string wrapper).

  2. Storage: C returns a pointer into static read-only data; TS returns a fresh JS string literal each call. Indistinguishable to the caller -- the string is identity-stable across calls.

Version-string source-of-truth: the golden_driver links the SYSTEM libmpfr.so (Ubuntu libmpfr-dev currently 4.2.1) NOT the cloned mpfr/ source (4.3.0-dev). When upgrading the system libmpfr-dev OR the cloned mpfr/, the TS port string literal must be re-aligned to match the system .so. Calibration will catch any drift.
refs: mpfr/src/version.c L24-L28 -- the C reference body.
